title: Catching Up After a Long Time
context: Running into an old friend or relative after a long absence.

lines.speaker: A
lines.arabic: مرحبا والله! مش شايفك من زمان، كيفك؟
lines.transliteration: Marḥaba wallah! Mish shāyfak min zamān, kīfak?
lines.english: Wow hello! Haven't seen you in ages, how are you?

lines.speaker: B
lines.arabic: منيح الحمد لله! صراحة مشغول كتير. شو أخبارك إنت؟
lines.transliteration: Mnīḥ il-ḥamdullah! Ṣarāḥa mashghūl ktīr. Shū akhbārak inta?
lines.english: Good, thank God! Honestly been really busy. Your news?

lines.speaker: A
lines.arabic: عم بدور على شغل جديد. يعني الوضع تعبان بس بتمنى يتحسن.
lines.transliteration: ʕam bdūr ʕala shughl jdīd. Yaʕni il-waḍ taʕbān bas btimanna yitḥassan.
lines.english: I'm looking for a new job. I mean, things are tough but hopefully improving.

lines.speaker: B
lines.arabic: إن شاء الله! أنت شاطر والله، رح تلاقي شي منيح. ما تقلق.
lines.transliteration: Inshallah! Inta shāṭer wallah, raḥ tlāʔi shi mnīḥ. Ma tʔlaʔ.
lines.english: God willing! You're capable, you'll find something good. Don't worry.

title: Talking About the Past
context: Reminiscing about childhood in Beirut with someone who shared those times.

lines.speaker: A
lines.arabic: بتذكر لما كنا صغار ببيروت؟
lines.transliteration: Btizakkar lamma kinna zghar b-Bayrūt?
lines.english: Do you remember when we were young in Beirut?

lines.speaker: B
lines.arabic: آه والله! كنا نتلهى بالزواريب كل يوم. ذكريات حلوة كتير.
lines.transliteration: Āh wallah! Kinna ntlahha biz-zawārib kull yōm. Zikrayāt ḥilwe ktīr.
lines.english: Oh yes! We used to play in the alleys every day. Such sweet memories.

lines.speaker: A
lines.arabic: والأكل! كانت إمي بتعمل الكبة كل يوم جمعة.
lines.transliteration: Wil-akl! Kānit immī btaʕmal il-kibbe kull yōm jumaʕa.
lines.english: And the food! My mom would make kibbeh every Friday.

lines.speaker: B
lines.arabic: يا ريت نرجع لهيك وقت. الحياة كانت أبسط.
lines.transliteration: Yā rēt nirjaʕ la-hēk waʔt. Il-ḥayāt kānit absat.
lines.english: If only we could go back to those times. Life was simpler.

title: Talking About Family
context: Describing family members, where they live, and what they do for a living.

lines.speaker: A
lines.arabic: قديش في عندك إخوة؟
lines.transliteration: ʔAddēsh fī ʕindak ikhwe?
lines.english: How many siblings do you have?

lines.speaker: B
lines.arabic: عندي أخ وختين. أخي الكبير عايش بالسعودية وختي الزغيرة لسّا بالجامعة.
lines.transliteration: ʕIndī akh w-ikhtēn. Akhi il-kbīr ʕāyish bis-Saʕūdiyye w-ikhti iz-zghīre lissa bil-jāmʕa.
lines.english: I have a brother and two sisters. My older brother lives in Saudi and my younger sister is still at university.

lines.speaker: A
lines.arabic: وختك التانية شو بتعمل؟
lines.transliteration: W-ikhtik it-tānye shū btaʕmal?
lines.english: And your other sister, what does she do?

lines.speaker: B
lines.arabic: هيي معلمة بمدرسة ببيروت. متجوزة وعندها ولدين.
lines.transliteration: Hiyye mʕallme b-madrase b-Bayrūt. Mjawwaze w-ʕindha waladēn.
lines.english: She's a teacher at a school in Beirut. Married with two kids.

lines.speaker: A
lines.arabic: حلو! وأهلك وينن هلق؟ لسا بالضيعة؟
lines.transliteration: Ḥilu! W-ahlak wēnon hallaʔ? Lissa biḍ-ḍayʕa?
lines.english: Nice! And where are your parents now? Still in the village?

lines.speaker: B
lines.arabic: إي، بيي وإمي لسا بالضيعة. ما بيحبو المدينة. بيقولو كتير ضجة.
lines.transliteration: Ē, bayyi w-immi lissa biḍ-ḍayʕa. Mā byiḥibbu il-mdīne. Biʔūlo ktīr ḍajje.
lines.english: Yes, my dad and mom are still in the village. They don't like the city. They say it's too noisy.

lines.speaker: A
lines.arabic: معن حق! أنا كمان بشتاق للضيعة. الهوا والأكل الطازج...
lines.transliteration: Maʕon ḥaʔʔ! Ana kamān bishtāʔ laḍ-ḍayʕa. Il-hawa wil-akl iṭ-ṭāzij...
lines.english: They're right! I also miss the village. The air and the fresh food...

lines.speaker: B
lines.arabic: إي والله. كل ما بروح بقلن خذوني معكن! بس الشغل ببيروت.
lines.transliteration: Ē wallah. Kill mā brūḥ bʔillon khudūni maʕkon! Bas ish-shughl b-Bayrūt.
lines.english: Honestly yes. Every time I visit I tell them take me with you! But work is in Beirut.

title: Sharing Childhood Memories
context: Two friends reminiscing about growing up, school, and their neighborhood.

lines.speaker: A
lines.arabic: بتتذكر مدرستنا القديمة؟ مرقت من قدّامها مبارح.
lines.transliteration: Btitzzakkar madrастna il-ʔadīme? Mrʔet min ʔuddāmha mbāriḥ.
lines.english: Do you remember our old school? I passed by it yesterday.

lines.speaker: B
lines.arabic: كيف لأ! كنا نلعب بالساحة كل الفرصة. والأستاذ فادي كان يزعق علينا!
lines.transliteration: Kīf laʔ! Kinna nilʕab bis-sāḥa kull il-furṣa. Wil-ustāz Fādi kān yuzʕuʔ ʕalēna!
lines.english: Of course! We used to play in the yard every recess. And Mr. Fadi would yell at us!

lines.speaker: A
lines.arabic: هههه إي! وبتتذكر لما كنا نشتري سندويشة فلافل من أبو حسين بعد المدرسة؟
lines.transliteration: Hahaha ē! W-btitzzakkar lamma kinna nishtiri sandwīshet falāfil min Abu Ḥusēn baʕd il-madrase?
lines.english: Hahaha yes! And remember when we'd buy falafel sandwiches from Abu Hussein after school?

lines.speaker: B
lines.arabic: أطيب فلافل بالحارة كلها! كان يعطينا زيادة لأنو يعرف بيي.
lines.transliteration: Aṭyab falāfil bil-ḥāra kullha! Kān yaʕṭīna zyāde laʔanno yaʕrif bayyi.
lines.english: The best falafel in the whole neighborhood! He'd give us extra because he knew my dad.

lines.speaker: A
lines.arabic: وبالصيف كنا نروح عالبركة ونقعد لحد ما يعتم.
lines.transliteration: W-biṣ-ṣayf kinna nrūḥ ʕal-birke w-nuʔʕud la-ḥadd mā yiʕtim.
lines.english: And in summer we'd go to the pool and stay until it got dark.

lines.speaker: B
lines.arabic: وإمي كانت تعصب لأنو كنت أرجع عتمان. كانت تقلي وين كنت يا حيوان!
lines.transliteration: W-immi kānit tiʕṣab laʔanno kint irjaʕ ʕatmān. Kānit tiʔillī wēn kint ya ḥaywān!
lines.english: And my mom would get angry because I'd come back after dark. She'd say 'where were you, you animal!'

lines.speaker: A
lines.arabic: هيك كانت الأمهات! بس هلق منشتاق لهالأيام.
lines.transliteration: Hēk kānit il-ummahāt! Bas hallaʔ mnishtāʔ la-hal-ayyām.
lines.english: That's how moms were! But now we miss those days.

lines.speaker: B
lines.arabic: أيام ما كان في همّ. لا شغل، لا فواتير، لا مسؤولية. بس لعب وأكل.
lines.transliteration: Ayyām mā kān fī hamm. Lā shughl, lā fawātīr, lā masʔūliyye. Bas laʕb w-akl.
lines.english: Days with no worries. No work, no bills, no responsibility. Just playing and eating.

title: Expressing Opinions About Food
context: Two friends debating which restaurant in Beirut has the best food. Food is central to Lebanese social life and everyone has strong opinions.

lines.speaker: Rami
lines.arabic: بتعرف شو، أحسن مطعم مشاوي ببيروت هوّي عند أبو ربيع. ما في متلو.
lines.transliteration: Btaʕrif shū, aḥsan maṭʕam mashāwi b-Bayrūt huwwe ʕind Abu Rabīʕ. Mā fī mitlo.
lines.english: You know what, the best grill restaurant in Beirut is Abu Rabiʕ's place. Nothing compares.

lines.speaker: Lina
lines.arabic: شو عم تحكي! يعني إم الشريف بتعمل كبة نيّي بتجنّن. ما حدا بيقدر عليها.
lines.transliteration: Shū ʕam taḥki! Yaʕni Imm ish-Sharīf btaʕmal kibbe nayyé btjannin. Mā ḥada byiʔdar ʕalēha.
lines.english: What are you saying! Imm el-Sharif makes raw kibbeh that's insane. Nobody can beat her.

lines.speaker: Rami
lines.arabic: الكبة النيّي شي وّالمشاوي شي تاني. بس إذا بدّك أطيب شيش طاووق، روحي عند أبو ربيع.
lines.transliteration: Il-kibbe in-nayyé shi wil-mashāwi shi tāni. Bas iza biddik aṭyab shīsh ṭāwūʔ, rūḥi ʕind Abu Rabīʕ.
lines.english: Raw kibbeh is one thing and grills are another. But if you want the best shish tawook, go to Abu Rabiʕ.

lines.speaker: Lina
lines.arabic: ماشي، بس أنا برأيي أحسن أكل هوّي اللي بتعملو الأمهات بالبيت. ولا مطعم بيقدر ينافس.
lines.transliteration: Māshi, bas ana braʔyi aḥsan akl huwwe illi btaʕmlo il-ummahāt bil-bēt. Wala maṭʕam byiʔdar ynāfis.
lines.english: Fine, but in my opinion the best food is what mothers make at home. No restaurant can compete.

lines.speaker: Rami
lines.arabic: هيدي معك حق فيها مية بالمية. أكل إمي ما إلو حل.
lines.transliteration: Haydi maʕik ḥaʔʔ fīha miyye bil-miyye. Akl immi mā ilo ḥall.
lines.english: You're a hundred percent right about that. My mom's food is unbeatable.

lines.speaker: Lina
lines.arabic: يلّا خلّينا نروح نتغدّا سوا هالأسبوع. أنا بختار المطعم هالمرّة!
lines.transliteration: Yalla khallīna nrūḥ nitghadda sawa hal-usbūʕ. Ana bikhtar il-maṭʕam hal-marra!
lines.english: Come on, let's go have lunch together this week. I'm picking the restaurant this time!

title: Telling a Funny Story
context: A friend narrates something embarrassing that happened to them, using past tense. Storytelling is a huge part of Lebanese social gatherings.

lines.speaker: Nadia
lines.arabic: لازم إحكيلك شو صار معي مبارح. والله فضيحة!
lines.transliteration: Lāzim iḥkīlak shū ṣār maʕi mbāriḥ. Wallah faḍīḥa!
lines.english: I have to tell you what happened to me yesterday. Honestly, a disaster!

lines.speaker: Sami
lines.arabic: شو صار؟ إحكيلي بسرعة!
lines.transliteration: Shū ṣār? Iḥkīli b-surʕa!
lines.english: What happened? Tell me quick!

lines.speaker: Nadia
lines.arabic: كنت بالسوبرماركت، وشفت واحد قلت هيدا صاحبي جورج. رحت ضربتو على كتفو وقلتلو كيفك يا حبيبي!
lines.transliteration: Kint bis-sūpermarkēt, w-shift wāḥad ʔilt hayda ṣāḥbi Jōrj. Riḥt ḍarabto ʕala katfo w-ʔiltillo kīfak yā ḥabībi!
lines.english: I was at the supermarket, and I saw someone I thought was my friend George. I went and slapped him on the shoulder and said 'how are you darling!'

lines.speaker: Sami
lines.arabic: لأ! وطلع مش هوّي؟
lines.transliteration: Laʔ! W-ṭiliʕ mish huwwe?
lines.english: No! And it turned out it wasn't him?

lines.speaker: Nadia
lines.arabic: والله طلع زلمة غريب! طلّع فيّي وقلّي مدام أنا ما بعرفك. حسّيت حالي بدّي موت من الخجل!
lines.transliteration: Wallah ṭiliʕ zalame gharīb! Ṭallaʕ fiyyi w-ʔilli madām ana mā baʕrfik. Ḥassēt ḥāli biddi mūt min il-khajal!
lines.english: It turned out to be a total stranger! He looked at me and said 'ma'am, I don't know you.' I felt like I could die of embarrassment!

lines.speaker: Sami
lines.arabic: هههههه يا حرام! والله شي يموّت من الضحك. شو عملتي بعدين؟
lines.transliteration: Hahahaha yā ḥarām! Wallah shi ymawwit min iḍ-ḍaḥk. Shū ʕmilti baʕdēn?
lines.english: Hahahaha poor you! That's hilarious. What did you do after?

lines.speaker: Nadia
lines.arabic: ركضت يعني! تركت الغراض وطلعت من السوبرماركت. ما بقدر أرجع لهونيك!
lines.transliteration: Rkaḍit yaʕni! Tarakt il-ghrāḍ w-ṭliʕt min is-sūpermarkēt. Mā biʔdar irjaʕ la-hunīk!
lines.english: I ran! Left my stuff and walked out of the supermarket. I can't go back there!

title: Making Weekend Plans
context: Two friends trying to decide what to do over the weekend. Lebanese weekends often involve mountains, beach, or long family lunches.

lines.speaker: Karim
lines.arabic: شو ناوي تعمل هالويكاند؟ في شي بالبال؟
lines.transliteration: Shū nāwi taʕmal hal-wīkānd? Fī shi bil-bāl?
lines.english: What are you planning to do this weekend? Got anything in mind?

lines.speaker: Maya
lines.arabic: والله ما عندي شي. يعني ممكن نطلع عالجبل نتمشّى شوي. الطقس حلو.
lines.transliteration: Wallah mā ʕindi shi. Yaʕni mumkin niṭlaʕ ʕal-jabal nitmashsha shway. Iṭ-ṭaʔs ḥilu.
lines.english: Honestly I have nothing. I mean we could go up to the mountains for a walk. The weather is nice.

lines.speaker: Karim
lines.arabic: فكرة حلوة! وبعدين منوقّف عند هديك المحل اللي بيعملو منقوشة زعتر عالصاج.
lines.transliteration: Fikra ḥilwe! W-baʕdēn mnwaʔʔif ʕind hadīk il-maḥall illi byaʕmlo manʔūshet zaʕtar ʕaṣ-ṣāj.
lines.english: Nice idea! And then we stop at that place that makes zaatar manoushe on the saj.

lines.speaker: Maya
lines.arabic: آه جوعتني! بس يعني بتقدر تصحى بكّير السبت؟ بعرفك بتنام لبعد الضهر.
lines.transliteration: Āh jawwaʕtni! Bas yaʕni btiʔdar tiṣḥa bakkīr is-sabt? Baʕrfak bitnām la-baʕd iḍ-ḍuhur.
lines.english: Ah you're making me hungry! But can you actually wake up early on Saturday? I know you sleep till afternoon.

lines.speaker: Karim
lines.arabic: لأ هالمرّة غير. بحطّ عشر منبّهات إذا لازم. الساعة تمانة منيح؟
lines.transliteration: Laʔ hal-marra ghēr. Bḥuṭṭ ʕashir mnabbihāt iza lāzim. Is-sāʕa tmēne mnīḥ?
lines.english: No, this time is different. I'll set ten alarms if I have to. Is eight o'clock good?

lines.speaker: Maya
lines.arabic: ماشي! يلّا بشوفك السبت. بس إذا تأخرت أنا رايحة بلاك.
lines.transliteration: Māshi! Yalla bshūfak is-sabt. Bas iza taʔakhkhart ana rāyḥa balāk.
lines.english: Deal! See you Saturday then. But if you're late I'm going without you.

title: Phone Conversation with Mom
context: The classic Lebanese mom phone call — checking if you ate, when you're visiting, and guilt-tripping. A universally relatable experience.

lines.speaker: Mama
lines.arabic: ألو حبيبي! وينك؟ ليش ما عم تردّ عالتلفون؟ قلقتني!
lines.transliteration: Alo ḥabībi! Wēnak? Lēsh mā ʕam trudd ʕat-talafōn? ʔalaʔtini!
lines.english: Hello darling! Where are you? Why aren't you answering the phone? You worried me!

lines.speaker: Tarek
lines.arabic: يا ماما كنت بالشغل! ما فيني إحكي كل ساعة.
lines.transliteration: Yā māma kint bish-shughl! Mā fīni iḥki kull sēʕa.
lines.english: Mom I was at work! I can't talk every hour.

lines.speaker: Mama
lines.arabic: ماشي ماشي. تغدّيت؟ أكيد ما أكلت شي. عم تنحف كتير.
lines.transliteration: Māshi māshi. Tghaddēt? Akīd mā akalt shi. ʕAm tinḥaf ktīr.
lines.english: Fine fine. Did you have lunch? I'm sure you didn't eat anything. You're getting too thin.

lines.speaker: Tarek
lines.arabic: أكلت ماما، أكلت! ساندويش من الدكّان.
lines.transliteration: Akalt māma, akalt! Sandwīsh min id-dikkēn.
lines.english: I ate mom, I ate! A sandwich from the shop.

lines.speaker: Mama
lines.arabic: ساندويش؟ هيدا مش أكل! تعا عالبيت الأحد عم بعمل ملوخيّة وفرّوج مشوي. ومحشي كوسا كمان.
lines.transliteration: Sandwīsh? Hayda mish akl! Taʕa ʕal-bēt il-aḥad ʕam baʕmal mulūkhiyye w-farrūj mashwi. W-maḥshi kūsa kamēn.
lines.english: A sandwich? That's not food! Come home Sunday, I'm making molokhia and grilled chicken. And stuffed zucchini too.

lines.speaker: Tarek
lines.arabic: إن شاء الله ماما. بحاول مرق بعد الضهر.
lines.transliteration: Inshallah māma. Bḥāwil murʔ baʕd iḍ-ḍuhur.
lines.english: God willing mom. I'll try to pass by in the afternoon.

lines.speaker: Mama
lines.arabic: شو يعني بتحاول؟ تعا وما تتأخّر! وجيب معك خبز من الفرن.
lines.transliteration: Shū yaʕni btḥāwil? Taʕa w-mā titʔakhkhar! W-jīb maʕak khubz min il-firin.
lines.english: What do you mean you'll try? Come and don't be late! And bring bread from the bakery.

lines.speaker: Tarek
lines.arabic: حاضر يا ماما. يلّا بحبّك. باي!
lines.transliteration: Ḥāḍir yā māma. Yalla bḥibbik. Bāy!
lines.english: Sure thing mom. Love you. Bye!

title: Discussing a TV Show
context: Two friends chatting about a Lebanese TV series they have been watching. Lebanese dramas and comedies are popular across the Arab world.

lines.speaker: Hiba
lines.arabic: شفتي الحلقة الأخيرة من المسلسل مبارح بالليل؟
lines.transliteration: Shifti il-ḥalʔa il-akhīra min il-musalsal mbāriḥ bil-lēl?
lines.english: Did you watch the last episode of the series last night?

lines.speaker: Dalia
lines.arabic: لأ لسا! ما تحكيلي شو صار، ما بدّي سبويلر!
lines.transliteration: Laʔ lissa! Mā tiḥkīli shū ṣār, mā biddi sboyler!
lines.english: Not yet! Don't tell me what happened, I don't want spoilers!

lines.speaker: Hiba
lines.arabic: بس والله الحلقة كانت مجنونة! يعني ما توقّعت هالشي أبداً.
lines.transliteration: Bas wallah il-ḥalʔa kānit majnūne! Yaʕni mā twaqqaʕt hal-shi abadan.
lines.english: But honestly the episode was crazy! I mean I didn't expect that at all.

lines.speaker: Dalia
lines.arabic: خلص خلص! بشوفها الليلة. بس قلّيلي، التمثيل كان منيح؟
lines.transliteration: Khalaṣ khalaṣ! Bshūfha il-lēle. Bas ʔillīli, it-tamthīl kān mnīḥ?
lines.english: Stop stop! I'll watch it tonight. But tell me, was the acting good?

lines.speaker: Hiba
lines.arabic: كتير! هيدي الممثّلة الجديدة والله شاطرة. بتحسّي إنّو حقيقي مش تمثيل.
lines.transliteration: Ktīr! Haydi il-mumaththile ij-jdīde wallah shāṭra. Btḥissi inno ḥaʔīʔi mish tamthīl.
lines.english: Very! That new actress is really talented. You feel like it's real, not acting.

lines.speaker: Dalia
lines.arabic: يلّا الليلة بحط البيجاما وبتفرّج. خبّريني بكرا منحكي عنها.
lines.transliteration: Yalla il-lēle bḥuṭṭ il-bījāma w-bitfarraj. Khabbriīni bukra minḥki ʕanha.
lines.english: Alright, tonight I'll put on my pajamas and watch. Text me tomorrow and we'll talk about it.

title: At a Wedding
context: Socializing at a Lebanese wedding, which typically involves lavish food, dabke dancing, and lots of comments about who's next to get married.

lines.speaker: Rima
lines.arabic: شو هالعرس الحلو! العروس كتير حلوة الله يسعدها.
lines.transliteration: Shū hal-ʕirs il-ḥilu! Il-ʕarūs ktīr ḥilwe alla yisʕidha.
lines.english: What a beautiful wedding! The bride is so pretty, God bless her.

lines.speaker: Jad
lines.arabic: إي والله. والأكل يا ريتك شايفة! مازات وكبّة ومشاوي ما بتخلص.
lines.transliteration: Ē wallah. Wil-akl yā rētak shēyfe! Mazzāt w-kibbe w-mashāwi mā btikhlaṣ.
lines.english: Absolutely. And the food, you should see! Mezze, kibbeh, and grills nonstop.

lines.speaker: Rima
lines.arabic: هلق بتجي خالتي تقلّي إمتى دورك يا ريما؟ كل عرس نفس القصّة.
lines.transliteration: Hallaʔ btiji khālti tiʔilli ēmta dawrak yā Rīma? Kull ʕirs nafs il-ʔiṣṣa.
lines.english: Now my aunt is going to come ask me 'when is your turn, Rima?' Same story at every wedding.

lines.speaker: Jad
lines.arabic: هههه قلّيلها إنشالله قريب وخلص الموضوع. يلّا تعي نرقص دبكة!
lines.transliteration: Hahaha ʔillīlha inshalla ʔarīb w-khalaṣ il-mawḍūʕ. Yalla taʕi nurʔuṣ dabke!
lines.english: Hahaha just tell her hopefully soon and end the conversation. Come on, let's dance dabke!

lines.speaker: Rima
lines.arabic: يلّا! بس خلّيني إلبس صبّاطي المريح أوّل. مش رح إقدر أرقص بهالكعب.
lines.transliteration: Yalla! Bas khallīni ilbas ṣabbāṭi il-mrīḥ awwal. Mish raḥ iʔdar urʔuṣ b-hal-kaʕb.
lines.english: Let's go! But let me put on my comfortable shoes first. I can't dance in these heels.

lines.speaker: Jad
lines.arabic: وبعد الدبكة لازم ناخد صورة مع العريس والعروس. ما منرجع بلا صور!
lines.transliteration: W-baʕd id-dabke lāzim nēkhod ṣūra maʕ il-ʕarīs wil-ʕarūs. Mā mnirjaʕ bala ṣuwar!
lines.english: And after the dabke we have to take a photo with the bride and groom. We can't leave without photos!

title: Asking for Help Moving
context: Asking a close friend for help moving apartments. In Lebanon, friends and family are the first resort for moving help before hiring professionals.

lines.speaker: Omar
lines.arabic: يا صاحبي بدّي أطلب منّك معروف كبير. بس ما تزعل.
lines.transliteration: Yā ṣāḥbi biddi uṭlub minnak maʕrūf kbīr. Bas mā tizʕal.
lines.english: My friend, I need to ask a big favor. But don't be upset.

lines.speaker: Fadi
lines.arabic: قول ما تستحي! شو في؟
lines.transliteration: ʔūl mā tistaḥi! Shū fī?
lines.english: Go ahead, don't be shy! What's up?

lines.speaker: Omar
lines.arabic: عم بنقل بيت السبت الجاي. بتقدر تساعدني أشيل الغراض؟ عندي كنبايات وتخت كبير.
lines.transliteration: ʕAm binʔul bēt is-sabt ij-jēy. Btiʔdar tsēʕidni ashīl il-ghrāḍ? ʕIndi kanabāyēt w-takht kbīr.
lines.english: I'm moving apartments next Saturday. Can you help me carry stuff? I have couches and a big bed.

lines.speaker: Fadi
lines.arabic: ما في مشكلة أبداً! بس جيب معك شباب تانيين كمان، أنا ضهري ما بيتحمّل.
lines.transliteration: Mā fī mushkile abadan! Bas jīb maʕak shabāb tānyīn kamēn, ana ḍahri mā byitḥammal.
lines.english: No problem at all! But bring other guys too, my back can't handle it alone.

lines.speaker: Omar
lines.arabic: أكيد! طلبت من خالد وعلي كمان. وبعد ما نخلص بعزمكن عمنقوشة وبيرة.
lines.transliteration: Akīd! Ṭalabt min Khālid w-ʕAli kamēn. W-baʕd mā nkhaliṣ baʕzimkon ʕa-manʔūshe w-bīra.
lines.english: Sure! I asked Khaled and Ali too. And after we finish I'll treat you all to manoushe and beer.

lines.speaker: Fadi
lines.arabic: هلق حكيت! هيدي أحلى جملة سمعتها اليوم. بشوفك السبت الصبح.
lines.transliteration: Hallaʔ ḥkīt! Haydi aḥla jumle smiʕtha il-yōm. Bshūfak is-sabt iṣ-ṣubuḥ.
lines.english: Now you're talking! That's the best sentence I heard today. See you Saturday morning.

title: Complaining About Traffic
context: Beirut's traffic is legendary for its chaos. Complaining about it is practically a national pastime and a universal bonding experience.

lines.speaker: Tony
lines.arabic: أخي قعدت ساعة ونص عالطريق من الأشرفية لحمرا. والله تجنّنت!
lines.transliteration: Akhi ʔaʕadit sēʕa w-nuṣṣ ʕaṭ-ṭarīʔ min il-Ashrafiyye la-Ḥamra. Wallah tjannant!
lines.english: Bro, I sat an hour and a half on the road from Ashrafieh to Hamra. I literally went crazy!

lines.speaker: Zeina
lines.arabic: ولا تقلّي! أنا مبارح أخدت ساعتين عالأوتوستراد. كان في حادث وسكّروا الطريق.
lines.transliteration: Wala tʔilli! Ana mbāriḥ akhadt sēʕtēn ʕal-ōtōstrād. Kān fī ḥēdis w-sakkaro iṭ-ṭarīʔ.
lines.english: Don't even tell me! Yesterday it took me two hours on the highway. There was an accident and they closed the road.

lines.speaker: Tony
lines.arabic: والزمامير ما بتوقّف! واحد ورايي ضلّو يزمّر عشر دقايق. يعني وين بدّي روح؟
lines.transliteration: Wiz-zamāmīr mā btwaʔʔaf! Wāḥad warāyi ḍallo yzammir ʕashir daʔāyiʔ. Yaʕni wēn biddi rūḥ?
lines.english: And the honking never stops! Someone behind me kept honking for ten minutes. Where am I supposed to go?

lines.speaker: Zeina
lines.arabic: هيك هنّي اللبنانيّة! بيفكرو الزمّور بيحلّ السير. والباركينغ، ما تحكيني!
lines.transliteration: Hēk hinne il-libnāniyye! Byifkiro iz-zammūr byiḥill is-sēr. Wil-bārkīng, mā taḥkīni!
lines.english: That's how Lebanese people are! They think honking solves traffic. And parking, don't get me started!

lines.speaker: Tony
lines.arabic: صح! دوّرت نص ساعة عموقف. بالآخر ركنت بمحل ممنوع وأخدت مخالفة!
lines.transliteration: Ṣaḥḥ! Dawwart nuṣṣ sēʕa ʕa-mawʔaf. Bil-ākhir rkant b-maḥall mamnūʕ w-akhadt mukhālafe!
lines.english: Right! I circled for half an hour looking for parking. In the end I parked in a no-parking zone and got a ticket!

lines.speaker: Zeina
lines.arabic: يلّا شو بدّنا نعمل. هيدي بيروت. لازم نتعوّد أو نفلّ من البلد!
lines.transliteration: Yalla shū biddna naʕmal. Haydi Bayrūt. Lāzim nitʕawwad aw nfill min il-balad!
lines.english: Well, what can we do. This is Beirut. We either get used to it or leave the country!

title: Planning a Birthday Party
context: Two friends organizing a surprise birthday party. Lebanese celebrations are known for going all out with food, decorations, and gatherings.

lines.speaker: Sara
lines.arabic: هلق عيد ميلاد ريما الأسبوع الجاي. لازم نعملّها سوربرايز!
lines.transliteration: Hallaʔ ʕīd mīlēd Rīma il-usbūʕ ij-jēy. Lāzim naʕmillha sorbrayze!
lines.english: So Rima's birthday is next week. We have to throw her a surprise!

lines.speaker: Ali
lines.arabic: فكرة حلوة! وين بدّنا نعملها؟ ببيتي في محل.
lines.transliteration: Fikra ḥilwe! Wēn biddna naʕmilha? B-bēti fī maḥall.
lines.english: Great idea! Where should we do it? My place has room.

lines.speaker: Sara
lines.arabic: تمام! أنا بجيب الكاتو والبالونات. إنت شوف مين بيقدر يجي.
lines.transliteration: Tamām! Ana bjīb il-kāto wil-bālōnāt. Inta shūf mīn byiʔdar yīji.
lines.english: Perfect! I'll bring the cake and balloons. You figure out who can come.

lines.speaker: Ali
lines.arabic: ماشي. بعملّها غروب عالواتساب بس من دونها. المهم ما حدا يفضحنا!
lines.transliteration: Māshi. Baʕmillha grūb ʕal-wātsāb bas min dūnha. Il-muhimm mā ḥada yifḍaḥna!
lines.english: Got it. I'll make a WhatsApp group without her. The important thing is nobody gives it away!

lines.speaker: Sara
lines.arabic: بالزبط! وبدّنا أكل كمان. بطلب بيتزا ومنقوشة. وأنت جيب المشروبات.
lines.transliteration: Biz-zabṭ! W-biddna akl kamēn. Buṭlub bītza w-manʔūshe. W-inta jīb il-mashrūbāt.
lines.english: Exactly! And we need food too. I'll order pizza and manoushe. And you bring the drinks.

lines.speaker: Ali
lines.arabic: وكيف بدّنا نجيبها عالبيت بلا ما تشكّ؟
lines.transliteration: W-kīf biddna njībha ʕal-bēt bala mā tshikk?
lines.english: And how are we going to get her to the house without her getting suspicious?

lines.speaker: Sara
lines.arabic: بقلّها تعي نتغدّا سوا. وهيي لما بتوصل بنقلّها سوربرايز! بتموت من الفرحة.
lines.transliteration: Bʔillha taʕi nitghadda sawa. W-hiyye lamma btwṣal bniʔillha sorbrayze! Btmūt min il-farḥa.
lines.english: I'll tell her come let's have lunch together. And when she arrives we yell surprise! She'll die of happiness.

lines.speaker: Ali
lines.arabic: يلّا خلص اتّفقنا. بلّشنا نحضّر من بكرا!
lines.transliteration: Yalla khalaṣ ittafaʔna. Ballashna nḥaḍḍir min bukra!
lines.english: Alright, it's a plan. Let's start preparing tomorrow!

title: Talking About the Weather
context: Discussing seasonal weather in Lebanon, where you can ski in the mountains and swim at the beach on the same day. Weather often determines weekend plans.

lines.speaker: Nour
lines.arabic: شو هالبرد اليوم! يعني مش طبيعي. حاسّة حالي بالثلّاجة.
lines.transliteration: Shū hal-bard il-yōm! Yaʕni mish ṭabīʕi. Ḥēsse ḥāli bit-tallēje.
lines.english: It's so cold today! I mean, this isn't normal. I feel like I'm in a fridge.

lines.speaker: Wael
lines.arabic: إي هلق بنص كانون! شو بدّك؟ بكرا في تلج عالجبل.
lines.transliteration: Ē hallaʔ b-nuṣṣ Kānūn! Shū biddik? Bukra fī talj ʕal-jabal.
lines.english: Well yeah, it's the middle of January! What do you expect? Tomorrow there's snow in the mountains.

lines.speaker: Nour
lines.arabic: والله؟ يلّا نروح نتزلّج! من زمان ما رحنا عالأرز.
lines.transliteration: Wallah? Yalla nrūḥ nitzallaj! Min zamān mā ruḥna ʕal-Arz.
lines.english: Really? Let's go skiing! It's been a while since we went to the Cedars.

lines.speaker: Wael
lines.arabic: بس الطريق بتخوّف بالتلج. لازم سلاسل عالدواليب.
lines.transliteration: Bas iṭ-ṭarīʔ btkhawwif bit-talj. Lāzim salāsil ʕad-dawālīb.
lines.english: But the road is scary in the snow. You need chains on the tires.

lines.speaker: Nour
lines.arabic: ما تقلق أنا بسوق. وبعد التزلّج منشرب سحلب ساخن عند المحل هونيك.
lines.transliteration: Mā tiʔlaʔ ana bsūʔ. W-baʕd it-tazalluj minshrab saḥlab sākhin ʕind il-maḥall hunīk.
lines.english: Don't worry, I'll drive. And after skiing we'll have hot sahlab at that shop there.

lines.speaker: Wael
lines.arabic: ماشي! وبالربيع بنروح عالبحر. هيدا أحلى شي بلبنان — تلج وبحر بنفس البلد!
lines.transliteration: Māshi! W-bir-rabīʕ bnirūḥ ʕal-baḥr. Hayda aḥla shi b-Lubnān — talj w-baḥr b-nafs il-balad!
lines.english: Deal! And in spring we'll go to the beach. That's the best thing about Lebanon — snow and sea in the same country!
